title: Profile segment membership against numeric variables
summary: Given a memberships CSV (respondent × solutions) and two numeric data sources (segmentation inputs and profiling inputs) with metadata, computes per-segment weighted means, weighted Welch t-scores vs the full sample, standardised differences, indices, and counts. Writes a per-solution xlsx workbook with conditional formatting plus a CSV sidecar per solution.
description: Reproduces the `profile_fast` pipeline: left-joins memberships onto the numeric inputs on `uuid_col`, stacks `profile_meta` over `seg_meta` (profile rows override duplicates), and for each solution column produces a long profiling table keyed to variable metadata (order/type/group/battery/final_variable/label). Per-segment statistics include wgt_mean, unweighted and weighted counts, weighted proportion, Welch t-score against the rest of the sample, standardised difference using the pooled unweighted SD, and an index (segment mean / overall mean × 100). Thresholds for the xlsx conditional formatting are exposed as live cells in the top-left of each sheet so analysts can adjust Count/Tscore/Sdiff/Index cutoffs without re-running the plugin.
usage:
[["seg-profile::profile"]]
memberships = "<memberships.csv>"
seg_inputs = "<seg_inputs.csv>"
seg_meta = "<seg_meta.csv>"
profile_inputs = "<profile_inputs.csv>"
profile_meta = "<profile_meta.csv>"
uuid_col = "resp_id"
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "p1"
# optional:
solution_cols = ["sol_3", "sol_4"]
weight_col = "wgt"
weight_source = "memberships"

arguments.name: memberships
arguments.description: CSV of respondent × solutions. Must contain `uuid_col` plus one column per solution whose values are the segment labels.

arguments.name: seg_inputs
arguments.description: CSV with `uuid_col` and the numeric variables used to build the segmentation.

arguments.name: seg_meta
arguments.description: Variable metadata for seg_inputs. Columns: variable, order, type, group, battery, final_variable, label.

arguments.name: profile_inputs
arguments.description: CSV with `uuid_col` and the numeric variables to profile against the segmentation. May overlap seg_inputs; duplicates are resolved in favour of profile_inputs.

arguments.name: profile_meta
arguments.description: Variable metadata for profile_inputs (same columns as seg_meta). Rows whose `variable` matches a seg_meta row override it.

arguments.name: uuid_col
arguments.description: Respondent identifier column. Must be present in memberships, seg_inputs, and profile_inputs.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run; no '..', '/' or '\\'.

arguments.name: solution_cols
arguments.description: Subset of solution columns to profile. Empty = all non-uuid columns in memberships.

arguments.name: weight_col
arguments.description: Name of a numeric weight column. Must be paired with `weight_source`.

arguments.name: drop_missing_segment
arguments.description: Drop respondents with a missing value in the active solution column before profiling.

arguments.name: na_action
arguments.description: How to handle NA values in numeric variables: `complete_cases` (per-variable pairwise deletion) or `fail` (abort).

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether CSV inputs have a header row.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Rayon worker threads for the per-variable profile loop. `1` (default) runs serially. Each variable is scored independently per solution, so parallel fan-out scales with the number of profile variables. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

notes: The conditional-formatting thresholds in the output xlsx (Count<, Tscore>, Sdiff>, Index>) are live cells. Editing them in Excel immediately re-highlights the table.
Weighted means use the supplied weight; standardised differences use unweighted SD in the denominator to match the R reference.

artifacts.title: Segment profile workbook
artifacts.description: One Excel workbook per segmentation solution. Each sheet profiles the segments against the survey variables: weighted means, Welch t-scores versus the total sample, standardised differences, and index values. Colour-coded cells highlight statistically meaningful differences. The formatting thresholds are live cells you can adjust in Excel without re-running.

fields.name: variable
fields.description: The survey variable or metric being profiled.

fields.name: seg_1 ... seg_K
fields.description: One column per segment showing the weighted mean for that variable in that segment.

fields.name: total
fields.description: The overall sample mean for context.

fields.name: tscore
fields.description: Welch t-statistic comparing each segment to the rest of the sample — a measure of how distinctive the segment is on this variable.

fields.name: sdiff
fields.description: Standardised difference (effect size) of the segment versus the total.

fields.name: index
fields.description: Segment mean as a percentage of the total mean (100 = average, 120 = 20% above average).

artifacts.title: Segment profile data
artifacts.description: The same profiling statistics in flat CSV format for further analysis or custom visualisation.
